Service 01

Marketing Audit

You suspect your marketing isn't working as hard as it should. But you're not sure where the gaps are, what's actually driving results, and what's just noise. We find out.

The challenge

You're spending.
But are you investing?

Marketing budgets keep growing. Headcount increases. New tools get added to the stack. But pipeline doesn't follow. The board asks why, and nobody has a clear answer. Not because the team isn't working hard. They are. But effort without alignment is just motion.

Teams stay busy executing campaigns, producing content, running events. Activity metrics look healthy. But when leadership asks what's actually producing revenue, the room goes quiet. There's a gap between what marketing does and what the business needs, and it widens a little more every quarter.

The real issue is rarely effort. It's focus. Without a clear picture of what's working and what isn't, every decision is a guess. And guessing gets expensive. Eventually, leadership loses confidence in marketing altogether. Not because marketing failed, but because nobody could prove it didn't.

What we deliver

A clear picture of what's working, what's wasted, and what to fix first.

Our audit is practical, not theoretical. You won't get a 90-page report full of best practices. You'll get an honest assessment of your current marketing, grounded in your data, your market, and your business goals.

Positioning & messaging review
How your brand shows up versus how it should. We assess whether your positioning is clear, differentiated, and consistent across every touchpoint your buyers encounter.
Channel & budget audit
Where money goes, what it produces, and what to cut or double down on. We map spend to outcomes so you can see which channels earn their keep and which are just burning budget.
Sales-marketing alignment assessment
How well the two functions connect. Where handoffs break. We look at lead definitions, SLAs, feedback loops, and shared metrics to find the friction points that cost you pipeline.
Prioritised recommendations with business impact
Not 50 ideas. The 5 that will move the needle. Each recommendation comes with expected impact, effort required, and a clear rationale tied to your business objectives.
How we work

From kickoff to recommendations in 3-4 weeks.

01
Discovery
Stakeholder interviews, data collection, access to tools and dashboards. We learn how your marketing works before we judge it.
Week 1
02
Analysis
Deep-dive into channels, content, pipeline data, competitor positioning. We follow the numbers and talk to the people who know the reality on the ground.
Week 2
03
Diagnosis
Pattern identification, gap analysis, opportunity mapping. This is where we connect what we found to what it means for your business.
Week 3
04
Recommendations
Presentation to leadership with a prioritised action plan. Clear, specific, and tied to business outcomes. No fluff, no filler.
Week 4
The numbers

The cost of flying blind.

65% of B2B marketing budgets are considered ineffective by the companies spending them Forrester 2024
$1T lost annually due to sales and marketing misalignment across B2B G2 / Industry 2024
27% faster profit growth in companies with tightly aligned marketing and sales ZoomInfo / Marketo 2024
Case in point

A manufacturing company cut marketing waste by 40% and redirected budget to channels that actually produced pipeline.

The company was spreading its marketing budget across eight channels with no clear attribution model. Every channel had a champion internally, and nobody could prove which ones were working. The result was a lot of activity, modest pipeline, and growing frustration from leadership about marketing ROI.

Our audit revealed that three of those eight channels drove roughly 80% of qualified leads. Two channels produced almost nothing measurable. The remaining three had potential but were underfunded and poorly executed. The problem wasn't the total budget. It was how it was distributed.

The company redirected spend, simplified the tech stack, and focused execution on the channels with proven results. Within a quarter, pipeline quality improved noticeably while total marketing spend actually decreased. The marketing team went from defending their budget to demonstrating clear contribution to revenue.
